title: HTTPS, TLS Identity, Certificate Trust, Proxies, and Downgrade Boundaries · Browser and Node Cli… · Inference Brief: Trust Roots Private Ca
summary: A universal browser-and-Node library must state what HTTPS trust it can configure in each runtime and where proxies or mTLS require Node boundaries. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts trust roots private ca increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch transport trust and certificate evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Add an intended private trust root or test certificate without replacing or weakening unrelated trust. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Preserve hostname, chain, validity, algorithm, and revocation-policy checks without disabling verification. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
